name: Maria
label: Executive assistant - first-time builder
background: Senior executive assistant at a 90-person SaaS company. Excellent with Google Workspace, Slack, calendars, and spreadsheets. New to terminal, Git, deployment, and database terms.
idea: Executive assistants lose track of action items after executive meetings because notes, email threads, Slack messages, and calendar follow-ups are scattered. I want an approval-based executive follow-up tracker that shows what needs attention today and drafts reminders without sending anything automatically.
target_user: executive assistants supporting busy founders and executives

name: Danielle
label: Executive assistant - confidentiality sensitive
background: Executive assistant to a CEO in healthcare services. Handles confidential meeting notes, inbox snippets, travel, and personnel-sensitive information. Comfortable with no-code tools but cautious about AI data sharing.
idea: Executive assistants need to prepare follow-up summaries from meeting notes, but many notes include confidential names, compensation details, health information, or board-sensitive topics. I want a private follow-up drafting tool that lets the EA redact sensitive details before AI sees them and keeps every draft approval-based.
target_user: executive assistants handling confidential executive workflows

name: Priya
label: Executive assistant - calendar and operations power user
background: EA and operations lead at a 150-person startup. Strong in spreadsheets, Airtable, Google Calendar, and process docs. Wants to build but will abandon if setup feels too developer-centric.
idea: EAs manually chase meeting prep and post-meeting follow-ups across recurring leadership meetings. I want a meeting operations dashboard that tracks recurring meeting prep, missing pre-reads, follow-ups, and reviewable reminder drafts.
target_user: executive assistants and operations coordinators managing recurring leadership meetings
likely_blockers: not wanting to touch deployment details
